requirements.full_sentence:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
<image>
RBI/DOR/2025-26/309
DOR.MRG.REC.No.228/00-00-011/2025-26


November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation
and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025 (Updated as on May
18, 2026)
Table of Contents
requirements.object: Contents

requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter I: Preliminary
Explanation: In this context, rural co-operative banks shall mean State Co-
operative Banks and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National
Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.
requirements.object: Banks and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Classification, Valuation, and Operation of Investment Portfolio)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation, and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect from the date of issue.
requirements.object: the date of issue

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as 'RCB') operating in India.
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as 'RCB') operating in India

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the
terms herein shall bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Current or Valid Credit Rating' for the purpose of determining rated
security means a credit rating granted by a credit rating agency in India,
registered with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) and
fulfilling the following conditions:
i The credit rating letter and rating rationale from the credit rating agency
shall preferably be part of offer document.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Current or Valid Credit Rating' for the purpose of determining rated security means a credit rating gra

requirements.full_sentence:
ii The credit rating letter shall not be more than one month old and rating
rationale shall not be more than one year old from the date of opening
of issue.
requirements.object: month old and rating rationale shall not be more than one year old from the date of opening of issue

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
iii In the case of secondary market acquisition, the credit rating of the
issue shall be in force and confirmed from the website of the respective
credit rating agency.
requirements.object: confirmed from the website of the respective credit rating agency

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Rated Security' means a security which carries current or valid credit
rating.
requirements.object: current or valid credit rating

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'Unrated Securities' means securities which do not have a current or
valid credit rating.
requirements.object: have a current or valid credit rating

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act (As applicable
to Co-operative Societies), the Reserve Bank of India Act (RBI Act), rules /
regulations made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act (As applicable to Co-operative Societies), the Reserve Bank of In

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors (Board) shall approve the Investment Policy of an RCB
as specified in paragraph 14(1).
requirements.object: of an RCB as specified in paragraph 14(1)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the shifting of investments to / from the Permanent
Category as specified in paragraph 28.
requirements.object: investments to / from the Permanent Category as specified in paragraph 28

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall be responsible to ensure that appropriate risk management
systems are in place for capturing and analysing the risk in respect of non-SLR
investments and for taking remedial measures in time as specified in paragraph
81.
requirements.object: that appropriate risk management systems are in place for capturing and analysing the risk in respect of non-SLR investments

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall review the following aspects of non-SLR investment at least on
a half yearly interval as specified in paragraph 82.
requirements.object: of non-SLR investment at least on a half yearly interval as specified in paragraph 82

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve and conduct an annual review of the panel of brokers
with whom an RCB may undertake transactions as specified in paragraph 101.
requirements.object: annual review of the panel of brokers with whom an RCB may undertake transactions as specified in paragraph 101

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall be informed post facto of any breach of the upper aggregate
contract limit for individual brokers as specified in paragraph 103(4).
requirements.object: of any breach of the upper aggregate contract limit for individual brokers as specified in paragraph 103(4)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall review the investment portfolio of an RCB on a half-yearly
basis as on March 31st and September 30th, and ensure that the same is placed
before it within two months, i.e., by end-May and end-November respectively,
as specified in paragraph 109.
requirements.object: of an RCB on a half-yearly basis as on March 31st and September 30th, and ensure that the same is placed before it wit

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve any decision of an RCB to build up a higher
percentage of IFR up to 10 per cent depending on the size and composition of
its portfolio, as specified in paragraph 115(2).
requirements.object: an RCB to build up a higher percentage of IFR up to 10 per cent depending on the size and composition of its portfolio, as spe

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall undertake investment activities as per the terms and conditions
specified in these Directions.
requirements.object: per the terms and conditions specified in these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) An RCB shall adopt a comprehensive Investment Policy duly approved by
the Board.
requirements.object: Policy duly approved by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The Investment Policy shall, at the minimum, include:
i The investment criteria and objectives to be achieved while
undertaking investment transactions, keeping in view the various
regulatory / statutory guidelines and the RCB's own internal
requirements.
requirements.object: include: i The investment criteria and objectives to be achieved while undertaking investment transactions, keeping in view the vari

requirements.full_sentence:
vii Proper risk management systems for making investment in non-SLR
securities which shall include entry-level minimum credit ratings /
quality standards and industry-wise, maturity-wise, duration-wise,
and issuer-wise limits to mitigate the adverse impact of concentration
and liquidity risk.
requirements.object: ratings / quality standards and industry-wise, maturity-wise, duration-wise, and issuer-wise limits to mitigate the

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not undertake any transactions on behalf of Portfolio
Management Scheme (PMS) clients.
requirements.object: behalf of Portfolio Management Scheme (PMS) clients

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment proposals shall be subjected to same degree of credit analysis as
any loan proposal, irrespective of the fact that the proposed investments may
be in rated securities.
requirements.object: degree of credit analysis as any loan proposal, irrespective of the fact that the proposed investments may be in rated securities

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall make its own internal credit analysis and
credit rating even in respect of rated issues and shall not entirely rely on the
ratings of external credit rating agencies.
requirements.object: ratings of external credit rating agencies

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall ensure robust internal credit rating systems, including a
mechanism for regular (quarterly or half-yearly) tracking of the financial position
of the issuer to ensure continuous monitoring of the rating migration of the
issuers / issues.
requirements.object: rating systems, including a mechanism for regular (quarterly or half-yearly) tracking of the financial position of the i

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall settle the transactions in securities as per procedure prescribed
in notifications / directions / guidelines issued by the concerned regulator.
requirements.object: securities as per procedure prescribed in notifications / directions / guidelines issued by the concerned regulator

requirements.full_sentence: An RCB shall hold its investments in securities only in dematerialised form.
requirements.object: securities only in dematerialised form

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall classify its entire investment portfolio (including SLR securities
and non-SLR securities) under two categories, viz., 'Current' and 'Permanent'.
requirements.object: portfolio (including SLR securities and non-SLR securities) under two categories, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall decide the category of the investment at the time of acquisition
and the decision shall be recorded on the investment proposal.
requirements.object: the investment at the time of acquisition and the decision shall be recorded on the investment proposal

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities acquired with the intention to hold them up to maturity shall be
classified as 'Permanent Investments'.
requirements.object: Investments'

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments under Permanent Category shall not exceed 50 per cent of an
RCB's total investments.
requirements.object: of an RCB's total investments

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The 50 per cent limit stated above shall not be applicable on
investments in the Government Securities.
requirements.object: in the Government Securities
under Permanent Category: (1) SLR securities

requirements.full_sentence:
Profit on sale of investments from Permanent Category shall be first taken to
the Profit & Loss Account and thereafter shall be appropriated to the 'Capital
Reserve Account'.
requirements.object: the Profit & Loss Account and thereafter shall be appropriated to the 'Capital Reserve Account'

requirements.full_sentence:
The amount so appropriated shall be net of taxes and
amount required to be transferred to statutory reserves.
requirements.object: and amount required to be transferred to statutory reserves

requirements.full_sentence:
Loss on sale shall be
recognised in the Profit & Loss Account.
requirements.object: Profit & Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities acquired and held with the intention of reselling them in the short
term shall be classified as 'Current Investments'.
requirements.object: Investments'

conditions: Provided that all investments in non-SLR securities from July 14
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that all investments in non-SLR securities from July 14, 2016, shall be
classified under 'Current' category.
requirements.object: category

requirements.full_sentence: Profit or loss on sale of investments shall be taken to the income statement.
requirements.object: income statement

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall have the option to shift investments to / from Permanent
Category with the approval of the Board once a year.
requirements.object: shift investments to / from Permanent Category with the approval of the Board once a year

conditions:
Provided that such shifting shall be done at the beginning of the accounting
year
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such shifting shall be done at the beginning of the accounting
year.
requirements.object: beginning of the accounting year

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that additional shifting to / from the Permanent Category
shall not be done during the remaining part of that accounting year.
requirements.object: remaining part of that accounting year

conditions:
where market value of a security as on the date of transfer is less than
the carrying value in the books
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where market value of a security as on the date of transfer is less than
the carrying value in the books, potential losses shall be recognized prior to the
transfer of security from Current Category to Permanent Category.
requirements.object: the transfer of security from Current Category to Permanent Category

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments under Permanent Category need not be 'marked to market' and
shall be valued at cost.

conditions: Where the cost price is higher than the face value
requirements.full_sentence:
(1) Where the cost price is higher than the face value, the premium shall be
amortized over the remaining period of maturity of the security.
requirements.object: remaining period of maturity of the security

conditions: Where the cost price is less than the face value
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where the cost price is less than the face value, the difference shall be
ignored and shall not be amortized or taken to income account since the
amount represents unrealised gain.
requirements.object: to income account since the amount represents unrealised gain

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities under Current Category shall be valued scrip-wise and depreciation
shall be provided for.
requirements.object: depreciation shall be provided for

requirements.full_sentence: Net appreciation, if any, shall be ignored.

Net depreciation
required to be provided for in any one classification shall not be reduced on
account of net appreciation in any other classification.
requirements.object: of net appreciation in any other classification

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly net depreciation
for any classification in one category shall not be reduced from appreciation in
similar classification in another category.
requirements.object: in similar classification in another category

requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter VI: Market Value
The 'market value' for the purpose of periodical valuation of investments
included in the Current Category shall be as under:

The 'market value' for the quoted securities shall be the prices declared by the
Financial Benchmarks India Pvt.
requirements.object: by the Financial Benchmarks India Pvt

requirements.full_sentence:
For securities whose prices are not published by FBIL, market price of
the quoted security shall be as available from the trades / quotes on the
exchanges / reporting platforms / trading platforms authorized by RBI / SEBI
and prices declared by Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives
Association of India (FIMMDA).
requirements.object: the trades / quotes on the exchanges / reporting platforms / trading platforms authorized by RBI / SEBI and prices declared by Fi

requirements.full_sentence:
Central Government Securities
(1) Unquoted Central Government Securities shall be valued on the basis of
the prices / YTM rates put out by the FBIL.
requirements.object: basis of the prices / YTM rates put out by the FBIL

requirements.full_sentence: (2) Treasury Bills shall be valued at carrying cost.
requirements.object: cost

requirements.full_sentence:
State Government Securities
State Government Securities shall be valued on the basis of the prices / YTM
rates put out by FBIL.
requirements.object: basis of the prices / YTM rates put out by FBIL

requirements.full_sentence:
Other Approved Securities
Other approved securities shall be valued applying the YTM method by marking
it up by 25 basis points above the yields of the Central Government Securities
of equivalent maturity put out by FBIL.
requirements.object: YTM method by marking it up by 25 basis points above the yields of the Central Government Securities of equivalent maturity put

requirements.full_sentence:
Debentures / Bonds of AIFIs and PSUs
All debentures / bonds other than debentures / bonds which are in the nature of
advance should be valued on the YTM basis.
requirements.object: YTM basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Such debentures / bonds may be
<image>
of different ratings.
requirements.object: ratings

requirements.full_sentence:
These shall be valued with appropriate mark-up over the
YTM rates for Central Government Securities as put out by FIMMDA
periodically.
requirements.object: mark-up over the YTM rates for Central Government Securities as put out by FIMMDA periodically

requirements.full_sentence:
The mark-up shall be graded according to the ratings assigned to
the debentures / bonds by the rating agencies subject to the following:
(1) The rate used for the YTM for rated debentures / bonds should be, at
least, 50 basis points above the rate applicable to a Government of India
bond of equivalent maturity.
requirements.object: the ratings assigned to the debentures / bonds by the rating agencies subject to the following: (1) The rate used for the YTM f

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The rate used for the YTM for un-rated debentures / bonds shall not be
less than the rate applicable to rated debentures / bonds of equivalent
maturity.
requirements.object: rate applicable to rated debentures / bonds of equivalent maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
The mark-up for the un-rated debentures / bonds shall
appropriately reflect the credit risk borne by the bank.
requirements.object: risk borne by the bank

conditions: Where interest / principal on the debenture / bonds is in arrears
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Where interest / principal on the debenture / bonds is in arrears, the
provision shall be made for the debentures as in the case of debentures /
bonds treated as advances.
requirements.object: debentures as in the case of debentures / bonds treated as advances

conditions:
where the interest is in arrears or principal is not paid
as per due date
requirements.full_sentence:
The depreciation / provision requirement
towards debentures where the interest is in arrears or principal is not paid
as per due date, shall not be allowed to be set off against appreciation
against other debentures / bonds.
requirements.object: set off against appreciation against other debentures / bonds

conditions:
Where the debentures / bond is quoted and there have been transactions
within 15 days prior to the valuation date
requirements.timeline: within 15 days prior to the valuation date
requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Where the debentures / bond is quoted and there have been transactions
within 15 days prior to the valuation date, the value adopted shall not be
higher than the rate at which the transaction is recorded on the stock
exchange.
requirements.object: rate at which the transaction is recorded on the stock exchange

conditions: If a RCB has regularly received dividends from co-operative institutions
requirements.full_sentence:
Share of Co-operative Institutions
If a RCB has regularly received dividends from co-operative institutions, then
their shares shall be valued at face value.
requirements.object: value

requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases,
the RCB shall make full provision in respect of its investments in shares of such
co-operative institutions.
requirements.object: respect of its investments in shares of such co-operative institutions

conditions:
where the financial position of co-operative
institutions in whose shares RCB has made investments is not available
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the financial position of co-operative
institutions in whose shares RCB has made investments is not available, the
shares shall be valued at ₹1 per co-operative institution.
requirements.object: per co-operative institution

requirements.full_sentence:
Units of Mutual Fund
<image>
Investments in quoted Mutual Fund Units shall be valued as per stock
exchange quotations.
requirements.object: stock exchange quotations

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments in non-quoted Mutual Funds Units shall be
valued on the basis of the latest re-purchase price declared by the Mutual
Funds in respect of each particular Scheme.
requirements.object: basis of the latest re-purchase price declared by the Mutual Funds in respect of each particular Scheme

conditions:
In case of funds with a lock-in
period
where repurchase price / market quote is not available
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of funds with a lock-in
period, or where repurchase price / market quote is not available, units shall be
valued at NAV.

conditions: If NAV is not available
requirements.full_sentence:
If NAV is not available, then these shall be valued at cost, till
the end of the lock-in period.
requirements.object: till the end of the lock-in period

requirements.full_sentence:
Commercial Papers and Certificate of Deposits
Commercial papers and Certificate of Deposits shall be valued at the carrying
cost.
requirements.object: carrying cost

requirements.full_sentence:
Zero coupon bonds (ZCBs)
(1) ZCBs shall be valued in the books at carrying cost which shall be
computed by adding the acquisition cost and discount accrued at the rate
prevailing at the time of acquisition, which shall be marked to market with
reference to the market value.
requirements.object: books at carrying cost which shall be computed by adding the acquisition cost and discount accrued at the rate prevailing at the time

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In the absence of market value, the ZCBs shall be marked to market with
reference to the present value of the ZCBs.
requirements.object: with reference to the present value of the ZCBs

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The present value of the ZCB may be calculated by
discounting the face value using the 'Zero Coupon Yield Curve', with
appropriate mark up as per the zero-coupon spreads put out by FIMMDA /
FBIL.
requirements.object: the face value using the 'Zero Coupon Yield Curve', with appropriate mark up as per the zero-coupon spreads put out by FI

conditions: In case the RCB is still carrying the ZCB at acquisition cost
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the RCB is still carrying the ZCB at acquisition cost, the
discount accrued on the instrument shall be notionally added to the book
value of the bond, before marking it to market.
requirements.object: the book value of the bond, before marking it to market

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of investments in security receipts (SRs) and other instruments
issued by an Asset Reconstruction Company (ARCs), an RCB shall comply
with the requirements of Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: of Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Transactions in Government Securities shall be done through either Subsidiary
General Ledger (SGL) account or Gilt account opened with entities eligible to
open Constituent Subsidiary General Ledger (CSGL) account with RBI.
requirements.object: Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) account or Gilt account opened with entities eligible to open Constituent Subsidiary General Le

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of maintaining Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) required under
Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, read with Section 56 thereof,
an RCB shall refer to the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
While undertaking OTC transactions in Government Securities, an RCB shall
seek a scheduled commercial bank, a Primary Dealer (PD), a financial
institution, an UCB, an insurance company, a mutual fund, or a provident fund,
as a counterparty for its transactions.
requirements.object: bank, a Primary Dealer (PD), a financial institution, an UCB, an insurance company, a mutual fund, or a provident fund, as

requirements.full_sentence:
Preference should be given to direct
deals with such counter parties.
requirements.object: deals with such counter parties

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB should check prices from other banks
or PDs with whom it may be maintaining Gilt account.

An RCB shall be eligible to access Negotiated Dealing System - Order
Matching (NDS-OM) either through direct access or through indirect access in
terms of Reserve Bank of India (Access Criteria for NDS-OM) Directions, 2024.
requirements.object: Negotiated Dealing System - Order Matching (NDS-OM) either through direct access or through indirect access in terms of Reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB maintaining gilt account may seek access to web-based NDS-OM
module through the Primary Member (i.e., entity with which gilt account is
maintained) for online trading in Government Securities in the secondary
market in terms of circular FMRD.DIRD.07/14.03.007/2016-17 dated October
20, 2016, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: NDS-OM module through the Primary Member (i

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB having an SGL account shall not open / maintain a gilt account with a
CSGL account holder unless the RCB is permitted to maintain both SGL and
gilt account in terms of the 'Eligibility Criteria and Operational Guidelines for
opening and maintaining of Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts and
Constituents' Subsidiary General Ledger (CSGL) Accounts' (Notification no.
requirements.object: gilt account with a CSGL account holder unless the RCB is permitted to maintain both SGL and gilt account in terms of the 'Eligibili

requirements.full_sentence: An RCB shall not maintain more than one gilt account.
requirements.object: gilt account

conditions: In case an RCB has a gilt account with a bank
requirements.full_sentence:
In case an RCB has a gilt account with a bank, the RCB shall open a
designated funds account (for all gilt related transactions) with the same bank.
requirements.object: account (for all gilt related transactions) with the same bank

conditions:
In case an RCB has a gilt account with any of the eligible non-banking
institutions
requirements.full_sentence:
In case an RCB has a gilt account with any of the eligible non-banking
institutions, the particulars of the designated funds account (with a bank) shall
be intimated to that non-banking institution.
requirements.object: non-banking institution

requirements.full_sentence:
For transactions through SGL / gilt accounts, an RCB shall report / conclude
transactions on NDS-OM and clear / settle them through CCIL as central
counterparty.
requirements.object: on NDS-OM and clear / settle them through CCIL as central counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
The settlement of transactions in Government Securities shall be governed by
the instructions issued vide FMRD.DIRD.06/14.03.007/2014-15 dated March
20, 2015, and paragraph 2.7 of Master Direction - Operational Guidelines for
Primary Dealers, 2016.
requirements.object: instructions issued vide FMRD

conditions:
if completed through the securities / funds shortage handling procedure of
CCIL
requirements.full_sentence:
Any default in delivery of security / funds in an SGL sale / purchase transaction,
even if completed through the securities / funds shortage handling procedure of
CCIL, shall be reported to Public Debt Office (PDO), RBI immediately.
requirements.object: Debt Office (PDO), RBI immediately

requirements.full_sentence:
In case
of SGL bouncing, an RCB shall be liable to pay penalties as per circular
IDMD.DOD.17/11.01.01(B)/2010-11 dated July 14, 2010, as amended from
time to time.
requirements.object: penalties as per circular IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall have the option to submit bids in the competitive auctions for
issue of Government Securities as per guidelines prescribed in General
Notification for Sale and Issue of Government of India Securities (including
Treasury Bills and Cash Management Bills) issued vide
IDMD.2320/08.01.01/2024-25 dated March 27, 2025, as amended from time to
time.
requirements.object: submit bids in the competitive auctions for issue of Government Securities as per guidelines prescribed in General Notification for

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall also have the option to participate through non-competitive
bidding facility in auctions of:
(1) Dated Government of India Securities and Treasury Bills in terms of
scheme on 'Auction of Government Securities - Non-competitive Bidding
Facility to Retail Investors' issued vide circular CO.IDMD.GBD(P).
requirements.object: to participate through non-competitive bidding facility in auctions of: (1) Dated Government of India Securities and Treasury Bil

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall undertake Repo transactions (including reverse repo
transactions) subject to guidelines specified in Repurchase Transactions
(Repo) (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018.
requirements.object: reverse repo transactions) subject to guidelines specified in Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve Bank) Dir

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not undertake short sale transactions, i.e., it shall not hold an
oversold position in any security.
requirements.object: i

requirements.full_sentence:
A Scheduled StCB may undertake retailing of Government Securities with non-
bank clients, such as provident funds, non-banking financial companies, high
net worth individuals, etc., subject to the following conditions:
(1) It may freely buy and sell Government Securities on an outright basis at
the prevailing market prices without any restriction on the period between
sale and purchase;
requirements.object: Securities with non- bank clients, such as provident funds, non-banking financial companies, high net worth individuals, etc.

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Retailing of Government Securities shall be on the basis of ongoing
market rates / yield curve emerging out of secondary market transactions;
requirements.object: of ongoing market rates / yield curve emerging out of secondary market transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) No sale of Government Securities shall be effected by a bank unless it
hold securities in its SGL / CSGL Account;
requirements.object: bank unless it hold securities in its SGL / CSGL Account

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) Immediately on sale, the corresponding amount shall be deducted by the
bank from its investment accounts and also from its SLR assets;
requirements.object: bank from its investment accounts and also from its SLR assets

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) These transactions shall be looked into by the concurrent / statutory
auditors of the bank;
requirements.object: the concurrent / statutory auditors of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(6) The bank shall put in place adequate internal control checks /
mechanisms as advised by the RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: internal control checks / mechanisms as advised by the RBI from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall have the option to undertake transactions in Government
Securities through exchanges, in addition to the existing mode of dealing
through SGL / CSGL accounts with RBI or gilt accounts with the designated
entities.
requirements.object: undertake transactions in Government Securities through exchanges, in addition to the existing mode of dealing through SGL / CSGL a

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, an RCB shall have the option to open a demat account with a
bank depository participant (DP) of NSDL / CDSL or with SHCIL.
requirements.object: open a demat account with a bank depository participant (DP) of NSDL / CDSL or with SHCIL

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of an RCB shall also ensure that Investment Policy of the RCB
provides for operations being conducted in accordance with the norms laid
down by RBI / SEBI and the respective exchange.
requirements.object: Policy of the RCB provides for operations being conducted in accordance with the norms laid down by RBI / SEBI and the res

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall put in place appropriate internal control systems, install enabling
IT infrastructure, and adequate risk management systems to cater to exchange
trading and settlement.
requirements.object: internal control systems, install enabling IT infrastructure, and adequate risk management systems to cater to exchange tradi

requirements.full_sentence:
The back-office arrangement shall be able to easily
track trading on the NDS-OM / OTC market and on the exchanges for
settlement, reconciliation, and management reporting.
requirements.object: track trading on the NDS-OM / OTC market and on the exchanges for settlement, reconciliation, and management reporting

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall use only a SEBI registered broker who is authorized by the
permitted exchanges (NSE / BSE) to undertake transactions in Government
Securities for placing buy / sell orders, subject to the guidelines on transactions
done through brokers as provided in paragraphs 95 to 103 of these Directions.
requirements.object: registered broker who is authorized by the permitted exchanges (NSE / BSE) to undertake transactions in Government Securities for placing buy / sell orders

requirements.full_sentence:
A valid contract note indicating the time of execution shall be obtained from the
broker at end of day.
requirements.object: broker at end of day

requirements.full_sentence:
All transactions shall be monitored with a view to ensuring timely receipt of
funds and securities.
requirements.object: view to ensuring timely receipt of funds and securities

requirements.full_sentence:
Any delay or failure shall be promptly taken up with the
exchange / authorities concerned.
requirements.object: with the exchange / authorities concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
At the time of trade, securities shall be available with the RCB either in its SGL
or in the demat account with depositories.
requirements.object: RCB either in its SGL or in the demat account with depositories

requirements.full_sentence:
Any settlement failure on account of non-delivery of securities / non- availability
of clear funds shall be treated as SGL bouncing and the current penalties in
respect of SGL bouncing shall be applicable.
requirements.object: bouncing and the current penalties in respect of SGL bouncing shall be applicable
invest in the following instruments under its non-SLR portfolio: (1) "A" or equivalent and higher rated corporate bonds

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in addition to the minimum rating prescribed above and
comparable market yields for the residual duration, an RCB shall not
invest in deep discount / zero coupon bonds unless the issuer builds up a
sinking fund for all accrued interest and keeps it invested in liquid
investments / securities (Government Securities).
requirements.object: / zero coupon bonds unless the issuer builds up a sinking fund for all accrued interest and keeps it invested in liquid invest

requirements.full_sentence: Provided that investments in CDs shall be limited to those issued by a
requirements.object: issued by a

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment in CDs shall
be treated as inter-bank deposits and shall not be reckoned for computing
the limit on non-SLR investments prescribed in paragraph 71 below.
requirements.object: the limit on non-SLR investments prescribed in paragraph 71 below

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that an RCB shall ensure that it does not have disproportionate
exposure in any one scheme of a Mutual Fund or to any one Mutual Fund.
requirements.object: not have disproportionate exposure in any one scheme of a Mutual Fund or to any one Mutual Fund

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For investment in the share capital of a society situated
outside its Area of Operation, RCB should refer to the Reserve Bank of
India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, a StCB should refer to the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-
operative Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025, for investment in the
share capital of a society not having the entire state, in which the StCB is
registered, as its Area of Operation.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co- operative Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025, for investment in the share capital of a society not

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not invest in the following non-SLR instruments:
(1) Perpetual Debt Instruments;
requirements.object: non-SLR instruments: (1) Perpetual Debt Instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments in non-SLR securities shall be limited to 10 per cent of the RCB's
total deposits as on March 31st of the previous year.
requirements.object: per cent of the RCB's total deposits as on March 31st of the previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment in unlisted non-SLR securities shall be subject to a minimum rating
prescribed at paragraph 69(1) and (2) above and shall not exceed 10 per cent
of the RCB's total non-SLR securities as on March 31st of the previous year.
requirements.object: of the RCB's total non-SLR securities as on March 31st of the previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that investments in non-SLR securities (both primary and secondary
market) by a RCB where the security is proposed to be listed in the
Exchange(s) shall be considered as investment in listed security at the time of
making investment.
requirements.object: in listed security at the time of making investment

conditions:
In case such security is not listed within the period
specified between issuance and listing
requirements.full_sentence:
In case such security is not listed within the period
specified between issuance and listing, the same shall be reckoned for the 10
per cent limit specified for unlisted non-SLR securities.
requirements.object: 10 per cent limit specified for unlisted non-SLR securities

conditions:
In case such
investments included under unlisted non-SLR securities lead to a breach of 10
per cent limit
requirements.full_sentence:
In case such
investments included under unlisted non-SLR securities lead to a breach of 10
per cent limit, the RCB shall not make further investments in non-SLR
securities (both primary and secondary market) till such time its investment in
unlisted non-SLR securities comes within the limit of 10 per cent.
requirements.object: non-SLR securities (both primary and secondary market) till such time its investment in unlisted non-SLR securities comes

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment in shares of co-operative societies shall be subject to the following
limits and conditions:
(1) The total investments of an RCB in the shares of co-operative institutions
shall not exceed 2 per cent of its owned funds (paid-up share capital and
reserves).
requirements.object: of its owned funds (paid-up share capital and reserves)

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The investment of an RCB in the shares of any one co-operative
institution coming under (1) above shall not exceed 5 per cent of the
subscribed capital of that institution.
requirements.object: of the subscribed capital of that institution

conditions:
When more than one co-operative bank (UCB or RCB)
contribute to the shares in a co-operative society
requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: When more than one co-operative bank (UCB or RCB)
contribute to the shares in a co-operative society, the limit of 5 per cent of
the subscribed capital indicated above shall apply in respect of the
investment of all the banks taken together, i.e., the total investment of all
the co-operative banks shall be limited to 5 per cent of the subscribed
capital of the enterprise concerned.
requirements.object: the investment of all the banks taken together, i

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An RCB shall invest in the shares of a co-operative society only if the by-
laws of the recipient society provide for the retirement of share capital
contributed by it.
requirements.object: of a co-operative society only if the by- laws of the recipient society provide for the retirement of share capital contributed b

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The retirement of the share capital contributed by an RCB to the shares of
any society shall be completed in 10 equal annual instalments
commencing from the financial year immediately following the year in
which the concern commences business or production.
requirements.object: equal annual instalments commencing from the financial year immediately following the year in which the concern commences business

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall have the option to exceed the limits prescribed in paragraphs 71,
72, and 73 in respect of following investments:
(1) Equity shares of Market Infrastructure Companies (MICs) if it becomes
necessary to do so for acquiring membership of these entities.
requirements.object: exceed the limits prescribed in paragraphs 71, 72, and 73 in respect of following investments: (1) Equity shares of Market Infrastr

requirements.full_sentence:
All non-SLR investments shall be subject to the prescribed prudential single /
group counter party exposure limits.
requirements.object: prescribed prudential single / group counter party exposure limits

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment in CPs shall be as per guidelines given in Master Direction -
Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of
original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions, 2024.
requirements.object: given in Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of original or initial maturit

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment in CDs shall be as per guidelines given in Master Direction -
Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021.
requirements.object: given in Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021

requirements.full_sentence:
Trades in listed corporate bonds shall be executed as per guidelines issued by
SEBI.
requirements.object: guidelines issued by SEBI

requirements.full_sentence:
All the secondary market OTC trades in corporate bonds shall be reported
within fifteen minutes of the trade on any of the exchanges.
requirements.object: minutes of the trade on any of the exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
All OTC trades in corporate bonds shall be cleared and settled through the
National Securities Clearing Corporation Ltd.
requirements.object: through the National Securities Clearing Corporation Ltd

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall be responsible to ensure that appropriate risk management
systems are in place for capturing and analysing the risk in respect of non-SLR
investments and for taking remedial measures in time.
requirements.object: that appropriate risk management systems are in place for capturing and analysing the risk in respect of non-SLR investments
of non-SLR investment at least on a half yearly interval: (1) Total business (investment and divestment) during the repor

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall ensure that credit facilities for activities / purposes precluded by
RBI regulations are not financed through investment in non-SLR securities.
requirements.object: for activities / purposes precluded by RBI regulations are not financed through investment in non-SLR securities

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall disclose the details of the issuer composition of non-SLR
investments and the non-performing investments, as per Reserve Bank of India
(Rural Co-operative Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and
Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: the issuer composition of non-SLR investments and the non-performing investments, as per Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-opera

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not deploy its funds in deposits with or loans to the State
Governments as doing so tantamount to indirect loaning to the State
Governments which is not the function of co-operative bank.
requirements.object: deposits with or loans to the State Governments as doing so tantamount to indirect loaning to the State Governments which is not t

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall desist from placing its surplus funds as deposits with an Urban
Co-operative Bank (UCB) as that tantamount to granting unsecured advances
to a UCB.
requirements.object: surplus funds as deposits with an Urban Co-operative Bank (UCB) as that tantamount to granting unsecured advances to a UCB

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall establish a robust internal control mechanism in respect of
investment transactions and shall, at a minimum, ensure the following:
requirements.object: control mechanism in respect of investment transactions and shall, at a minimum, ensure the following:

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a clear functional separation of (a) trading, (b) settlement, (c)
monitoring and control, and (d) accounting.
requirements.object: separation of (a) trading, (b) settlement, (c) monitoring and control, and (d) accounting

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a functional separation of trading and back-office functions
relating to RCB's own Investment Accounts and Other Constituents (including
brokers') Accounts.
requirements.object: of trading and back-office functions relating to RCB's own Investment Accounts and Other Constituents (including brokers')

requirements.full_sentence: Deal slips shall be prepared for every transaction entered into.
requirements.object: transaction entered into

conditions: if through a broker
requirements.full_sentence:
Deal slips shall
be serially numbered, properly accounted for and shall contain all the details of
the deal such as name of the counterparty, details of security, category (i.e.,
Current / Permanent) in which it shall be held, amount, price, contract date and
time, settlement date, confirmation mode to the counterparty, whether it is
direct deal or through a broker, and if through a broker, name of the broker,
brokerage payable etc.
requirements.object: accounted for and shall contain all the details of the deal such as name of the counterparty, details of security, cate

conditions:
where the requirement is waived off in terms of
circular IDMD.No.766/10.26.65A/2005-06 dated August 22
requirements.full_sentence:
Back-office shall monitor timely receipt of confirmation
from the counterparty, except where the requirement is waived off in terms of
circular IDMD.No.766/10.26.65A/2005-06 dated August 22, 2005, and circular
FMRD.FMID.01/14.01.02/2014-15 dated December 19, 2014.
requirements.object: confirmation from the counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
The books of accounts shall be updated independently, on the basis of
vouchers passed, after verification of actual contract notes received from the
broker / counterparty and confirmation of the deal by the counterparty.
requirements.object: the basis of vouchers passed, after verification of actual contract notes received from the broker / counterparty and con

conditions: if any, shall be properly recorded
requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 19 of these Directions, securities held in physical
form, if any, shall be properly recorded, held under joint custody, and shall be
subjected to quarterly verification by persons unconnected with their custody.
requirements.object: under joint custody, and shall be subjected to quarterly verification by persons unconnected with their custody

requirements.full_sentence:
Balances of Government Securities as per RCB's books shall be reconciled at
least at quarterly intervals with the balances in the books of the PDO.
requirements.object: at quarterly intervals with the balances in the books of the PDO

conditions:
If the
number of transactions so warrant
requirements.full_sentence:
If the
number of transactions so warrant, the reconciliation should be undertaken
more frequently, say monthly.
requirements.object: say monthly

requirements.full_sentence:
This reconciliation shall be periodically checked
by the internal audit department of the RCB.
requirements.object: the internal audit department of the RCB

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a system for reporting transactions to Top Management on a
weekly basis which shall include the details of transactions in securities, details
of SGL bouncing, and a review of investment transactions undertaken during
the period.
requirements.object: reporting transactions to Top Management on a weekly basis which shall include the details of transactions in securities, details of S

requirements.full_sentence: An RCB shall engage brokers on the terms and conditions specified hereunder:
requirements.object: terms and conditions specified hereunder:

requirements.full_sentence:
The role of the broker shall be restricted to that of bringing the two parties to the
deal together.
requirements.object: of bringing the two parties to the deal together

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not give power of attorney or any other
authorisation to the brokers / intermediaries to deal on its behalf in the money
and securities markets.
requirements.object: or any other authorisation to the brokers / intermediaries to deal on its behalf in the money and securities markets

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the same shall be
disclosed after it is decided to enter into the transaction to facilitate direct
settlement between counterparties.
requirements.object: is decided to enter into the transaction to facilitate direct settlement between counterparties

requirements.full_sentence: The brokers shall have no role in the process of settlement of deals.
requirements.object: the process of settlement of deals

requirements.full_sentence:
The
settlement of the deal shall take directly between the counterparties, viz., both
fund settlement and delivery of security.
requirements.object: counterparties, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall maintain a record of broker-wise details of deals put through and
brokerage paid.
requirements.object: broker-wise details of deals put through and brokerage paid

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not transact in Government Securities in physical form with any
broker.
requirements.object: in physical form with any broker

requirements.full_sentence: An RCB shall not engage services of any broker in inter-bank transactions.
requirements.object: broker in inter-bank transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the above prohibition shall not apply to an RCB undertaking
securities transactions among themselves through members of a recognised
stock exchange.
requirements.object: undertaking securities transactions among themselves through members of a recognised stock exchange

requirements.full_sentence:
The criteria for empanelment of brokers shall, at the minimum, include the
following:
<image>
(1) SEBI Registration;
requirements.object: include the following: <image> (1) SEBI Registration

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall ensure that aggregate transactions (both purchases and sales)
executed through an individual broker do not exceed 5 per cent of total
transactions through brokers in a financial year under review, subject to the
following conditions:
(1) The limit shall be observed with reference to the year under review and
the RCB shall keep in view the expected turnover of the current year
which shall be based on turnover of the previous year and anticipated rise
or fall in the volume of business in the current year.
requirements.object: (both purchases and sales) executed through an individual broker do not exceed 5 per cent of total transactions through brokers in a financial year under review

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The limit shall cover both the business initiated by RCB and the business
offered / brought by a broker.
requirements.object: initiated by RCB and the business offered / brought by a broker

conditions:
where no
brokers are involved
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Transactions entered into directly with counterparties, i.e., where no
brokers are involved, shall be excluded to arrive at the total transactions
through brokers.
requirements.object: at the total transactions through brokers

conditions:
If for any reason it becomes necessary to exceed the aggregate limit for
any broker
requirements.full_sentence:
(4) If for any reason it becomes necessary to exceed the aggregate limit for
any broker, the RCB shall record in writing the specific reasons for such
breach and the Board shall be informed post facto.
requirements.object: specific reasons for such breach and the Board shall be informed post facto

conditions: In case of repo transactions both the legs of the deals
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of repo transactions both the legs of the deals, i.e., purchase as
well as sale shall be included to arrive at the volume of total transactions.
requirements.object: at the volume of total transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The limit of 5 per cent shall not apply to transactions through Primary
Dealers.
requirements.object: Primary Dealers

requirements.full_sentence:
Treasury transactions shall be separately subjected to concurrent audit and the
results of their audit shall be placed before the Chief Executive of the RCB
once every month.
requirements.object: concurrent audit and the results of their audit shall be placed before the Chief Executive of the RCB once every month

requirements.full_sentence:
The business done through brokers shall be audited by the same concurrent
auditors who audit the treasury operations of the RCB, and the audit report
shall be included in the monthly report to the Chief Executive of the RCB.
requirements.object: same concurrent auditors who audit the treasury operations of the RCB, and the audit report shall be included in the monthly report

requirements.full_sentence:
The concurrent audit shall, at the minimum:
(1) Certify that investments held by the RCB as on the last reporting Friday of
each quarter and as reported to the RBI are actually owned / held by it in
SGL / CSGL / Gilt account / dematerialised account or in a physical form,
where applicable.
requirements.object: (1) Certify that investments held by the RCB as on the last reporting Friday of each quarter and as reported to the RBI are actually owned / held by it in SGL / CSGL / Gilt account / dematerialised account or in a physical form

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB not having the system of concurrent audit shall
have the above certification furnished by an auditor appointed by
Registrar of Co-operative Societies.
requirements.object: furnished by an auditor appointed by Registrar of Co-operative Societies

requirements.timeline:
within 30 days from the end of the relevant
quarter
requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The above Certificate shall be submitted to concerned
Regional Office of NABARD within 30 days from the end of the relevant
quarter.
requirements.object: Regional Office of NABARD within 30 days from the end of the relevant quarter

requirements.full_sentence:
The internal audit shall cover the transactions in securities on an ongoing basis,
monitor compliance with the laid down management policies / prescribed
procedures and report the deficiencies directly to the Top Management.
requirements.object: securities on an ongoing basis, monitor compliance with the laid down management policies / prescribed procedures and report

requirements.full_sentence:
The internal auditors / internal audit department shall periodically check the
reconciliation of SGL / CSGL account balance with PDO, as required in
paragraph 93 of these Directions.
requirements.object: of SGL / CSGL account balance with PDO, as required in paragraph 93 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall undertake a half-yearly review of its investment portfolio as on
March 31st and September 30th and place the same before its Board within two
<image>
months, i.e., by end-May and end-November.
requirements.object: of its investment portfolio as on March 31st and September 30th and place the same before its Board within two <image>

requirements.full_sentence:
The review shall, at the minimum,
cover operational aspects of investment portfolio including reconciliation of SGL
/ CSGL accounts with PDO, SGL bouncing, amendments made to the
Investment Policy, business put through any individual broker or brokers in
excess of the limit with the reasons therefor, irregularities observed in all audit
reports, position of compliance thereto, and certify adherence to laid down
internal Investment Policy and procedures and RBI's guidelines.
requirements.object: cover operational aspects of investment portfolio including reconciliation of SGL / CSGL accounts with PDO, SGL bouncing, amendments

requirements.full_sentence:
A return on 'Half-Yearly Review of Investment Portfolio' shall be submitted
through the ENSURE portal of NABARD by June 15 and December 15
respectively, in a format (Proforma as per Annex I) as advised by NABARD.
requirements.object: ENSURE portal of NABARD by June 15 and December 15 respectively, in a format (Proforma as per Annex I) as advised by NABARD

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall recognize income on accrual basis for the following:
(1) Government Securities and corporate bonds, where interest rates on the
instruments are predetermined provided the interest is serviced regularly
and is not in arrears.
requirements.object: basis for the following: (1) Government Securities and corporate bonds

requirements.full_sentence:
Income from units of mutual funds (debt mutual funds and money market
mutual funds) shall be booked on cash basis.
requirements.object: basis

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of investments in Government Securities and approved securities, an
RCB shall not capitalise the broken period interest paid to seller as part of cost
but treat it as an item of expenditure under Profit & Loss Account.
requirements.object: interest paid to seller as part of cost but treat it as an item of expenditure under Profit & Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: This accounting treatment does not take into account the tax
implications and an RCB shall comply with the requirements of income tax
authorities as prescribed.
requirements.object: of income tax authorities as prescribed

requirements.full_sentence:
Costs such as brokerage fees, commission or taxes incurred at the time of
acquisition of securities under Current Category shall be recognized as
expenses, without any accrual.
requirements.object: without any accrual

requirements.full_sentence:
Creation of IFR and Minimum Requirement
<image>
(1) 1An RCB shall maintain IFR of not less than 5 per cent of its investment
portfolio classified under the Current Category.
requirements.object: less than 5 per cent of its investment portfolio classified under the Current Category

requirements.full_sentence:
This minimum requirement
shall be assessed annually and shall be computed with reference to the
book value of its investments in the Current Category as of the balance
sheet date.
requirements.object: shall be computed with reference to the book value of its investments in the Current Category as of the balance sheet date

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An RCB may, at its discretion, build up a higher percentage of IFR up to
10 per cent depending on the size and composition of its portfolio, with the
approval of its Board.
requirements.object: build up a higher percentage of IFR up to 10 per cent depending on the size and composition of its portfolio, with the approval o

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An RCB shall build IFR out of realized gains on sale of investments, and
subject to available net profit.
requirements.object: realized gains on sale of investments, and subject to available net profit

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Accounting Treatment
i The provisions held on account of depreciation in the value of
investments held under Current Category shall be shown as
'Investment Depreciation Reserve (IDR)'.
requirements.object: Depreciation Reserve (IDR)'

conditions:
if any, and net of consequent
reduction in the transfer to Statutory Reserve) or the balance available
in the IFR Account
requirements.full_sentence:
IDR required to be created in
any year shall be debited to the Profit & Loss Account and an
equivalent amount (net of tax benefit, if any, and net of consequent
reduction in the transfer to Statutory Reserve) or the balance available
in the IFR Account, whichever is less, shall be transferred from the IFR
Account to Profit & Loss Account.
requirements.object: Profit & Loss Account and an equivalent amount (net of tax benefit

requirements.full_sentence:
ii In the event that the IDR created on account of depreciation in
investments is found to be in excess of the required amount in any
year, the excess shall be credited to the Profit & Loss Account and an
equivalent amount (net of taxes, if any, and net of transfer to Statutory
Reserves as applicable to such excess provision) shall be
appropriated to the IFR Account to be utilised to meet future
depreciation requirement for investments.
requirements.object: Profit & Loss Account and an equivalent amount (net of taxes

requirements.full_sentence:
iii The amounts debited to the Profit & Loss Account for depreciation
provision and the amounts credited to the Profit & Loss Account for
1 Substituted with effect from May 18, 2026, vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Classification, Valuation, and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated May 18,
2026.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation, and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Amendment Direction

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
reversal of excess provision shall be debited and credited respectively
under the head "Expenditure - Provisions & Contingencies".
requirements.object: respectively under the head "Expenditure - Provisions & Contingencies"

requirements.full_sentence:
The
amounts appropriated from the Profit & Loss Account and the amounts
transferred from the IFR to the Profit & Loss Account shall be shown
as 'below the line' items after determining the profit for the year.
requirements.object: the line' items after determining the profit for the year

requirements.full_sentence: (5) IFR shall be eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: in Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence:
Draw-down from IFR
(1) An RCB may, at its discretion, draw down the balance available in IFR in
excess of 5 per cent of its investment in Current Category for credit to the
balance of profit / loss as disclosed in the Profit and Loss Account at the
end of any accounting year.
requirements.object: draw down the balance available in IFR in excess of 5 per cent of its investment in Current Category for credit to the balance of

conditions:
Where the balance in the IFR is less than 5 per cent of its investment in
Current Category
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where the balance in the IFR is less than 5 per cent of its investment in
Current Category, a draw down shall be permitted subject to the following
conditions:
i The drawn down amount is used only for meeting the minimum Tier 1
capital requirements by way of appropriation to free reserves or
reducing the balance of loss, and
ii The amount drawn down shall not exceed the amount by which the
MTM provisions made during the aforesaid year exceed the net profit
on sale of investments during the same year.
requirements.object: which the MTM provisions made during the aforesaid year exceed the net profit on sale of investments during the same year

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Paragraph 117(1) above shall apply mutatis mutandis to preference
shares where the fixed dividend is not paid.
requirements.object: preference shares where the fixed dividend is not paid

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In the case of equity shares, where the investment in the shares of any
company is valued at ₹1 per company on account of the non-availability of
the latest Balance Sheet, such equity shares shall be classified as NPI.

conditions: If any credit facility availed by the issuer is NPA in the books of the bank
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) If any credit facility availed by the issuer is NPA in the books of the bank,
investment in any of the securities issued by the same issuer shall also be
treated as NPI.
requirements.object: NPI

conditions: where interest / principal is in arrears
requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of securities where interest / principal is in arrears, the RCB should
not reckon income on the securities and should also make appropriate
provisions for the depreciation in the value of the investment.
requirements.object: securities and should also make appropriate provisions for the depreciation in the value of the investment

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB should
not set-off the depreciation requirement in respect of these non-performing
securities against the appreciation in respect of other performing securities.
requirements.object: in respect of these non-performing securities against the appreciation in respect of other performing securities

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior
to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
<image>
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of
any provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
